title: 10 best herbal teas for gum infection

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Tea tree oil, which is often used to make tea tree teas, has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the mouth, including those that cause gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in tea tree tea may help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infection, making it a natural remedy for reducing discomfort.
Antioxidant property: Antioxidants found in tea tree tea can help protect the mouth from further damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and disease progression.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, teas helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea tree oil found in the leaves of this plant contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which have been shown to combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation in the gums. As a result, tea tree tea may help alleviate symptoms of gum infection, such as redness, swelling, and pain, promoting a healthier and more comfortable oral environment. Regular consumption can also support overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Cineole's antibacterial properties help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans.
Linalool: Linalool's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it easier to manage gum infections.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene's antimicrobial properties help fight against the bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, thereby promoting gum health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antimicrobial properties, which help combat gum infections.
Buds: Rich in essential oils, particularly cineole, that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: Also contain essential oils and flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh leaves and twigs of melaleuca alternifolia from a trusted source.
Chop 2-3 teaspoons of the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the chopped plant material in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove solids.
Store the cooled herbal tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, easing discomfort and pain associated with gum infections.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with gum infection because it possesses potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive compounds present in Echinacea angustifolia, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, have been shown to exhibit significant antibacterial and antifungal activity, effectively combating the growth of harmful microorganisms that cause gum infections. This natural remedy promotes oral health by reducing inflammation, alleviating pain, and accelerating the healing process of infected gums.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help combat the bacterial growth responsible for gum infections.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Echinacea angustifolia tea may contribute to its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Alkaloids: The alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia tea may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat infection and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are commonly used to make teas for gum infection because they contain high concentrations of compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Buds are used to make teas due to their content of chemical compounds that can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the gums.
Leaves: Leaves are utilized in teas for their ability to provide antioxidant properties, which can aid in healing and preventing gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh echinacea angustifolia flowers or 2-3 tablespoons of dried roots and flowers.
Chop the echinacea angustifolia flowers or roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers or 2 teaspoons of dried roots in 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to release the herbs' active compounds and flavor.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving the echinacea angustifolia tea hot or chilled.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Cloves contain eugenol, which has antimicrobial properties that can help kill bacteria that cause gum infections and reduce inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory property: Eugenol in cloves also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Analgesic property: The essential oil of cloves contains analgesic compounds that can help alleviate toothache and other gum-related pains, providing relief from discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum, also known as cloves, teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The eugenol present in cloves has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Candida albicans. This natural antiseptic also reduces inflammation and swelling in the gum tissue, promoting healing and preventing further infection. Regular consumption of Syzygium aromaticum tea may also help to prevent gum disease and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Eugenol is a phenolic compound that acts as an antibacterial agent, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene is a sesquiterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Beta-Caryophyllene: Beta-caryophyllene is a sesquiterpene that has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, helping to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their antibacterial properties, which help reduce gum inflammation and fight gum infections.
Buds: The buds are used to create a tea that reduces gum inflammation and helps to heal mouth ulcers and gum infections.
Leaves: The leaves of Syzygium aromaticum are used to make a tea that helps to reduce gum inflammation and fight gum infections due to their antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried syzygium aromaticum buds for every 8 oz of water.
Heat 8 oz of water in a saucepan over medium heat until it reaches 212°f.
Add the measured syzygium aromaticum buds to the hot water and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the buds.
Drink the herbal tea immediately while warm to enjoy its fragrance and flavor.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used in teas for gum infection due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Used in teas for gum infection due to their antimicrobial and antiseptic properties.
Roots: Used in teas for gum infection due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1/4 cup of dried calendula flowers for every 8 oz of water needed for the tea.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the mixture using a tea infuser or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids completely.

Antimicrobial properties: Wintergreen tea contains compounds like methyl salicylate, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans, helping to prevent further infection and promote healing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, teas helps with gum infection because of its high content of methyl salicylate, a compound similar to aspirin. The antibacterial properties of wintergreen tea also inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause infections, promoting a healthy environment for healing. remedy_parts: Leaves: Gaultheria procumbens leaves are commonly used to make teas for gum infection due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Gaultheria procumbens are used to create teas that help combat gum infection by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Buds: Gaultheria procumbens buds are another part used in teas for gum infection due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that aid in soothing and healing the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 20-30 grams of fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems.
Dry the harvested gaultheria procumbens in a low-temperature oven at 150-200 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Store the dried gaultheria procumbens in an airtight container to preserve its flavor and aroma.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried gaultheria procumbens and steep it in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain and discard the gaultheria procumbens leaves and serve the tea hot or chilled within 30 minutes.

Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried m x piperita leaves in the right amount for the desired tea strength about 1 teaspoon per 8 ounces of water.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 inches of fresh ginger root and peel it using a vegetable peeler.

Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with gum infection because it contains berberine, a natural antibiotic that effectively combats bacteria responsible for periodontitis and other oral infections. The antimicrobial properties of goldenseal tea help to reduce inflammation and kill the microorganisms that cause bad breath, bleeding gums, and swelling. As an anti-inflammatory agent, it also reduces pain and discomfort associated with gum infection, promoting a healthy mouth environment and preventing further complications.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine has antibacterial properties that can help combat the bacterial infection causing the gum infection, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Biosides: Goldenseal's biosides (a class of alkaloids) have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, as well as minimize bleeding and discomfort associated with gum infections.

Alkaloids: The alkaloids present in goldenseal tea have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the growth and spread of bacteria, fungi, and viruses, including those that can cause gum infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant are rich in berberine, a bioactive compound that has antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating gum infections.

Rhyzomes: Goldenseal's rhyzomes contain high concentrations of alkaloids, including berberine, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat microbial infections associated with gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried goldenseal root powder from a trusted source.
Measure two cups of boiling water and pour over the goldenseal powder in a tea infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to release active compounds and flavors.
Strain the liquid into a cup and add honey to taste as desired.
Drink the warm tea immediately, ideally twice daily for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with gum infection because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that effectively combat bacterial growth, reducing inflammation and swelling in the affected area. The soothing and calming effects of chamomile also help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gum infections. Additionally, its antiseptic properties aid in wound healing by promoting the formation of healthy tissue, ultimately promoting a speedy recovery from gum infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Chamomile tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation in gums, making it an effective natural remedy for treating gum infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can reduce swelling and redness in the gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with gum infections.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile tea's antioxidant content helps protect against oxidative stress and damage to gum tissue, promoting healthy gum function and reducing the risk of further infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are used to make tea as they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and soothe gums.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are commonly used in herbal teas for gum infections due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh flowers for each 8 oz cup of water.
Measure 200ml boiling water and pour it over the harvested flowers in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Steep the tea mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the chamomile to release its flavonoids and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids retaining 6 oz of the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down for 2-3 minutes before gargling and swishing it around your mouth to help soothe and disinfect gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with gum infection because of its antibacterial properties, which effectively combat bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce swelling and ease pain associated with infected gums. Additionally, dandelion's high water content helps to flush out toxins and debris from the mouth, promoting a healthy oral environment. As a result, regular consumption of dandelion teas may help alleviate symptoms of gum infection, such as redness, bleeding, and bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Dandelion tea contains compounds like taraxasterol and sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, helping to combat gum infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's flavonoid and phenolic acid content reduces inflammation in the mouth, which can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and redness associated with gum infection.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea, like caffeic acid and quercetin, neutralize free radicals that damage gum tissue and contribute to disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum infections.
Fruits: Containing prebiotic fibers, dandelion fruits support oral health by promoting a balanced gut microbiome, which can prevent gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and leaves to prepare for tea infusion.
Dry the harvested plant material in a cool dark place for at least two weeks.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale root per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herbal mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal teas are periodontal diseases, toothaches, and mouth sores. Teas made from herbs like sage, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the gums. Ginger tea is also used to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with toothaches and mouth sores, while peppermint tea helps freshen breath and reduce bad odors.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum infection are natural remedies that help alleviate the symptoms and promote healing of gum infections, also known as periodontal disease. They work by reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and improving oral health. The best herbal teas for gum infection include Echinacea purpurea, Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Salvia officinalis, Zingiber officinale, Calendula officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Sanguinaria canadensis, Achillea millefolium, and Aloe barbadensis. If left untreated, gum infections can lead to tooth loss, bad breath, and social isolation, negatively impacting one's quality of life and self-esteem.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with gum infection is peppermint, chamomile, and calendula. Peppermint tea's antibacterial properties help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, while chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the gums. Calendula tea's antiseptic properties promote healing and prevent infection. Drinking a combination of these teas can help alleviate symptoms, reduce pain, and promote oral health. It is essential to consult a dentist before using herbal teas as a treatment for gum infection.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Ayu, herbal teas made from certain plants, such as Ocimum sanctum (Tulsi) and Aloe vera, have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are beneficial in treating gum infections. The study involved sixty patients with gingivitis who were randomly allocated into three groups: Ocimum sanctum mouthwash, Aloe vera mouthwash, and chlorhexidine mouthwash. The results showed that Ocimum sanctum, Aloe vera, and chlorhexidine mouthwashes were equally effective in reducing plaque, gingival, and bleeding indices at a 30-day interval, indicating that herbal teas may be a good alternative for treating gum infections, especially when used in the long-term.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with gum infection because it stimulates the body's natural defense mechanisms, helping to fight off bacterial and fungal infections. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects boost the immune system, enhancing its ability to combat infection. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy immune response, Echinacea purpurea teas can aid in the treatment and prevention of gum infections, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: Help by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
Phenolic Acids: Assist in reducing inflammation and swelling in the gums, thereby alleviating symptoms of gum infections.
Flavonoids: Contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in healing and protecting the gums from further damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are a primary source of echinacoside and alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate gum infection symptoms.
Leaves: They contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacteria causing gum infections.
Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can aid in reducing gum infection inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers to make 1 gallon of tea.
Dry the harvested roots and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried roots and 1/4 cup of dried flowers with 1 quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then drink the tea within 24 hours for optimal benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial properties, which combat the bacteria responsible for the infection. The tea's active compound, cineole, has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help to prevent the spread of infection, allowing the gums to recover more effectively. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Melaleuca alternifolia tea provides relief from gum infections, promoting a healthy and pain-free oral environment. Regular consumption can lead to improved gum health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause gum infections.
Linalool: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, effectively killing the bacteria that contribute to gum disease and infections.
Cymene: Demonstrates potent antimicrobial activity, which helps combat the growth of bacteria that lead to gum infections and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high amounts of compounds like cymene and cineole, which have antimicrobial properties to help combat gum infection.
Stems: They contain essential oils that can help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria in the mouth, promoting healing of gum infections.

Buds: The buds of Melaleuca alternifolia contain a higher concentration of essential oils than other parts, making them an effective ingredient for treating gum infections due to their potent antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal flavor and aroma.
Chop 2 teaspoons of the harvested leaves into smaller pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Steep the chopped leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves and sediment from the liquid.
Store the prepared tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consuming to preserve its potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat bacteria that cause inflammation and infection. The tea's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and camphor, have a decongestant effect, reducing swelling and promoting blood flow to the affected area. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the gums, reducing pain and discomfort associated with gum infections. This natural remedy provides relief and promotes healing, making it an effective treatment for gum infections.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
Eugenol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Linalool: Demonstrates antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, helping to prevent the spread of infection and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of eucalyptol, a natural antibacterial agent that helps combat gum infections.
Barks: Used for their astringent properties, which help reduce inflammation and soothe gum infections.
Stems: Used due to their eucalyptol content, which helps kill bacteria and reduce swelling associated with gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Store the eucalyptus globulus tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days in an airtight container.
Serve the eucalyptus globulus tea hot or iced according to your personal preference immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis teas helps with gum infection because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, including Streptococcus and Escherichia. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has been found to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for treating gum infections and promoting overall oral health. Its antiseptic properties also help to prevent further infection.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins have antimicrobial properties, which help combat the bacterial growth that causes gum infection.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing gum inflammation and promoting healing.
Ursolic acid: Ursolic acid has antibacterial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria that lead to gum infection and promoting oral health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis are commonly used to make teas for gum infection because they contain antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections.

Flowers: The flowers of Salvia officinalis are sometimes used in teas for gum infection because they possess antimicrobial and antiseptic properties that aid in soothing and healing the affected area.
Buds: The buds of Salvia officinalis are occasionally used in teas for gum infection due to their antiseptic properties that help to combat bacterial growth and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 tablespoons of fresh salvia officinalis leaves or use 1 teaspoon of dried leaves for a cup.
Steep the leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves completely.
Add honey or sugar to taste and stir well to combine the flavors evenly now.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes before serving and consuming it immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacterial growth and inflammation in the gums. The active compound gingerol in Zingiber officinale tea has been shown to reduce swelling and kill bacteria that cause gum infections, such as periodontitis. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain and discomfort associated with gum infections, promoting a healthy and pain-free oral environment. Regular consumption can aid in preventing recurrent infections.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the gums, helping to alleviate symptoms of gum infection.
Shogaols: Possess antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria causing gum infection, promoting a healthy oral environment.
Volatile oils (particularly zingiberene and bisabolol): Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote wound healing in the gums.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most used part of Zingiber officinale for making teas to treat gum infection because they contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are also commonly used for making teas to treat gum infection due to their high content of gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation and fight infection.
Buds: Buds are sometimes used to make teas for gum infection as they contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried zingiber officinale root powder for every 8 ounces of water.
Heat the water to a boil in a teapot or kettle for 2 to 3 minutes.
Steep the dried zingiber officinale root powder in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature for drinking within 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis teas helps with gum infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Additionally, Calendula's ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi makes it effective in combatting gum infections caused by these microorganisms. The tea's soothing properties also promote healing and reduce inflammation in the affected area, contributing to a quicker recovery from gum infections.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in treating gum infections and preventing further complications.

Phenolic acids: Display antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infections, such as Streptococcus and Escherichia, thereby reducing the severity of the infection.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe swollen gums and prevent tissue damage, promoting a faster recovery from gum infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce gum inflammation and prevent infection.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, they possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and heal gum infections.
Roots: They contain sesquiterpene lactones, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating gum infections and promoting oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 flower petals of calendula officinalis in the late morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested petals in a low-temperature oven at 105°f for 2-3 hours to preserve their properties.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried calendula officinalis petals with 1 cup of boiling water to create the tea.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water slowly.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving the calendula officinalis tea to the patient.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas helps with gum infection because it improves blood flow to the affected area. The tea's antioxidant properties and anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and prevent further damage. Ginkgo biloba also enhances the healing process by promoting the growth of new tissue and improving the overall circulation. This, in turn, helps to reduce the severity of gum infection symptoms, such as redness, swelling, and pain, providing relief and promoting faster recovery. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba tea may also help to prevent future gum infections.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, thus promoting healthy gum tissue.
Terpenoids: They have antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to gum infections, helping to prevent the spread of infection.
Bilobalide: This constituent has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce gum inflammation and prevent tissue damage, promoting overall gum health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Ginkgo biloba are commonly used to make teas due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce gum inflammation.
Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves also contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may have antimicrobial properties that can help combat gum infections.
Leaves: The antioxidant properties of Ginkgo biloba leaves may also help protect the gums from further damage and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a tea infuser.
Heat 8 ounces of water in a saucepan over medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the hot water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the used tea leaves immediately.
Allow the tea to cool for 2 to 5 minutes before serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sanguinaria canadensis teas helps with gum infection because it possesses antimicrobial properties, which combat the bacteria that cause inflammation and infection. The tea's active compounds, including sanguinarine and chelerythrine, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a common cause of gum disease. Additionally, Sanguinaria canadensis teas contain anti-inflammatory agents that reduce swelling and pain, promoting healing and resolution of the infection. This natural remedy can provide relief from gum infection and promote oral health.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline alkaloids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with gum infections, promoting a conducive environment for healing.
Quinolizidine alkaloids: They exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, thus preventing further inflammation and infection.
Benzylisoquinoline alkaloids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected gum tissue, allowing for a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain sanguinarine, a compound that has antimicrobial properties, effective against gum infections.
Leaves: They also contain sanguinarine and other alkaloids, which help combat gum infections due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Like roots, rhyzomes contain sanguinarine, making them useful in teas for treating gum infections due to their antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of fresh sanguinaria canadensis roots in late summer or early fall when roots are mature.
Clean and dry the harvested roots to prevent contamination and preserve their medicinal properties immediately.
Chop 1-2 teaspoons of dried sanguinaria canadensis roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release their medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to enjoy a cup of sanguinaria canadensis herbal tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, work synergistically to combat bacterial growth and inflammation in the gums. This natural remedy reduces swelling, soothes discomfort, and promotes healing of the affected area. Regular consumption of Achillea millefolium tea has been shown to alleviate symptoms of gum infection, leading to a faster recovery and improved oral health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with gum infection by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which reduce swelling and prevent bacterial growth in the affected area.
Rosmarinic acid: It helps with gum infection by displaying antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions, which protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by bacterial infections.
Chamazulene: It helps with gum infection by showing antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which prevent bacterial growth and reduce inflammation in the gums, thus promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat gum infections.
Flowers: They are used to make teas because of their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infection in the gums.
Stems: They are used to make teas due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in quantities of 1 to 2 teaspoons per cup of water.
Boil 8 ounces of water for 3 to 5 minutes to reach a rolling boil.
Steep the gathered achillea millefolium in the boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool for a few minutes before serving and consuming immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas helps with gum infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, have been shown to reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause gum infections. Additionally, Aloe barbadensis contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which promote healing and soothe inflamed gums. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat various oral health issues, including gum infections, tooth decay, and mouth sores.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums.

Furanochromones: These compounds have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections and promote a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe barbadensis leaves are used to make teas for gum infection due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Leaves: Aloe barbadensis leaves are also used to make teas for gum infection due to their ability to promote wound healing and reduce swelling.
Leaves: The gel from Aloe barbadensis leaves is used to make teas for gum infection due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory effects on the gums.
Leaves: Aloe barbadensis leaves are used to make teas for gum infection due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Roots: Aloe barbadensis roots are used to make teas for gum infection due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat gum infection.
Leaves: The gel from Aloe barbadensis leaves is used to make teas for gum infection due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory effects on the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-150 grams of fresh aloe barbadensis leaves from a mature plant.
Chop the aloe leaves into small pieces and soak them in 1 liter of water for 2 hours.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a large bowl and discard the solids.
Boil the liquid in a saucepan over medium heat for 10-15 minutes to reduce the volume.
Let the tea cool to room temperature and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
